question:
Select the most appropriate synonym for the underlined word.
The officer was not aware of the <u>sedulous</u> espionage.
explanation: • **Sedulous**: Showing dedication and diligence. (Synonym: Diligent). ✍️

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
We may _______ an example or two.
explanation:
• **Cite**: To quote or refer to a form of evidence or example. 📜
• **Sight**: The faculty of seeing.
• **Site**: A specific location.

question:
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
I have waiting / anxiously / for my / exam results.
explanation:
• **Grammar Error**: The present perfect continuous tense requires 'been' between 'have' and the present participle.
• **Correction**: It should be 'I **have been waiting**'. ⏳

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
**Obedient**
explanation:
• **Obedient**: Complying or willing to comply with orders or requests. 😇
• **Disobedient**: Refusing to obey rules or someone in authority. (Antonym). 😠

question:
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.
**The study of ancient writings and scriptures**
explanation: • **Palaeography**: The study of ancient writing systems and the deciphering and dating of historical manuscripts. 📜🖋️

question:
Select the correct idiom to substitute the underlined segment.
When the teacher asked him about the homework, he <u>started speaking vaguely to avoid talking about it</u>.
options: searching the bird in the bush
explanation: • **Beat around the bush**: An idiom meaning to discuss a matter without coming to the point or to delay talking about the main issue. 🌳🗣️

question: Select the MISSPELT word.
explanation:
• **Incorrect spelling**: Farnish. ❌
• **Correct spelling**: **Garnish** (to decorate food) or **Varnish** (a protective resinous coating). ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate synonym for the underlined word.
Wordsworth wished to work on his poems in <u>solitude</u>.
explanation: • **Solitude/Seclusion**: The state of being alone or private, away from people. 🧘‍♂️

question:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment.
Rajiv <u>helps his friends of solve</u> real-life problems...
explanation: • **Infinitive pattern**: The verb 'help' can be followed by an object and a to-infinitive phrase ('helps someone **to do** something'). ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.
The businessman agreed to <u>grease the palm</u> of the bank manager.
explanation: • **Grease the palm**: An idiomatic expression meaning to give money to someone in exchange for an illicit favor; to bribe. 🤝💸

question:
Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word.
Late morning, we took a <u>stroll</u> along the beach.
explanation: • **Stroll/Saunter**: To walk in a slow, relaxed, and leisurely manner. (Synonyms). 🚶‍♂️🌊

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
The doctor instructed the patient to ______ up and down his house as an exercise.
explanation: • **Walk**: Moving at a regular pace on foot. Walking up and down a house is a standard light therapeutic exercise. 🚶‍♂️

question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
It is surprising to see _________ (stale) apples available throughout the year.
explanation:
• **Stale**: No longer fresh and pleasant to eat. 🍏
• **Fresh**: Recently produced or picked; not stale. (Antonym requested to complete the contextual logic). 🍎✨

question: Select the option in which all the words are spelt correctly.
options: My aunty arrivved at my home on the eve of Diwali.
My aunty arrived at my home on the eve of Diwali.
My aunty arived at my home on the eve of Diwali.
My aunty arriived at my home on the eve of Diwali.
answer: My aunty arrived at my home on the eve of Diwali.
explanation: • **Spelling Check**: Option 2 contains completely correct standard English spelling for the verb '**arrived**'. ✅

question:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.
The curry was very ________ (distasteful). I ate it all.
explanation:
• **Distasteful**: Unpleasant or completely unpalatable. 🤢
• **Delicious**: Highly pleasant to the taste. (Antonym explaining why they finished the plate). 🍛😋

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 1) The set of relationships between people, (1) __________ their social position...
explanation: • **Including**: Used to introduce sub-elements that form part of the whole human system. 👥

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 2) ...or even the (2) _________ world are referred to as a society.
explanation: • **Entire**: Whole or complete. It standardly collocates with 'world' to mean global society. 🌍

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 3) ...is a group of people bound together by functional (3) _________...
explanation: • **Interdependence**: The dependence of two or more people or things on each other; functional bonds in an organized civilization. 🤝

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 4) Patterns of relationships between individuals who (4) __________ a common culture...
explanation: • **Share**: To possess or experience something in common with others. Society is built on a shared culture. 🗺️

question: (Cloze Test - Blank 5) ...permits its members to fulfil wants that they could not (5) _________ on their own.
explanation: • **Accomplish**: Achieve or complete successfully. Working as an organized collective helps people complete massive tasks. 🏆
